There is some differences in cupyx.scipy's `lsmr()` and scipy's original `lsmr()` with their arguments.

CuPy
https://docs.cupy.dev/en/latest/reference/generated/cupyx.scipy.sparse.linalg.lsmr.html
```py
lsmr(A, b, x0=None, damp=0.0, atol=1e-06, btol=1e-06, conlim=100000000.0, maxiter=None)
```

SciPy
https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/generated/scipy.sparse.linalg.lsmr.html
```py
lsmr(A, b, damp=0.0, atol=1e-06, btol=1e-06, conlim=100000000.0, maxiter=None, show=False, x0=None)
```

I found the following differences:

- Position of `x0`.
- CuPy's lsmr does not have `show` argument.
- When `maxiter=None` (default behavior), SciPy lsmr sets `maxiter=min(m, n)` (where (m, n) is the shape of A) automatically while CuPy's does not do so.

Added [CUDA Graphs conditional nodes](https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/dynamic-control-flow-in-cuda-graphs-with-conditional-nodes/) support for CuPy with the _functional_ API.

CUDA Graphs conditional nodes can be used in CUDA 12.4+ and this feature adds data-dependent dynamic control flow such as while-like loop or if-like branch conditions into CUDA Graphs.

In summary, this PR includes the following changes:

1. Support for some low-level CUDA runtime API to use conditional nodes from CuPy.
2. Functional API to define CUDA Graph with conditional nodes

Added [CUDA Graphs conditional nodes](https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/dynamic-control-flow-in-cuda-graphs-with-conditional-nodes/) support for CuPy with the _functional_ API.

CUDA Graphs conditional nodes can be used in CUDA 12.4+ and this feature adds data-dependent dynamic control flow such as while-like loop or if-like branch conditions into CUDA Graphs.

This PR includes the following changes:

1. Support for some low-level CUDA runtime API to use conditional nodes from CuPy.
2. Functional API to define CUDA Graph with conditional nodes

CUDA runtime provides a useful debug function [`cudaGraphDebugDotPrint()`](https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-runtime-api/group__CUDART__GRAPH.html#group__CUDART__GRAPH_1gbec177c250000405c570dc8c4bde20db) for CUDA graphs.
This function can generate [DOT format](https://graphviz.org/doc/info/lang.html) file from `cudaGraph_t`.

This PR contains CuPy's wrapper of this function with the following APIs:

In `cupy.cuda.runtime`, 
- `graphDebugDotPrint(graph, path, flags)` function
- [`cudaGraphDebugDotFlags`](https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-runtime-api/group__CUDART__TYPES.html#group__CUDART__TYPES_1g0907ca7a1e7d0211b71ee49c5403072b) enums


In `cupy.cuda.Graph` class,
- `debug_dot_str(self, flags) -> str`: function to get debug DOT string

## Usage example

```python
import cupy
x = cupy.array([0, 1, 2, 3, 4])
y = cupy.array([5, 6, 7, 8, 9])
st = cupy.cuda.Stream()
with st:
    st.begin_capture()
    x = x + y
    g = st.end_capture()

print(g.debug_dot_str(cupy.cuda.runtime.cudaGraphDebugDotFlagsVerbose))

Abstract

CUDA runtime provides a useful debug function [`cudaGraphDebugDotPrint()`](https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-runtime-api/group__CUDART__GRAPH.html#group__CUDART__GRAPH_1gbec177c250000405c570dc8c4bde20db) for CUDA graphs.
This function can generate [DOT format](https://graphviz.org/doc/info/lang.html) file from `cudaGraph_t`.

This PR contains CuPy's wrapper of this function with the following APIs:

In `cupy.cuda.runtime`, 
- `graphDebugDotPrint(graph, path, flags)` function
- [`cudaGraphDebugDotFlags`](https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-runtime-api/group__CUDART__TYPES.html#group__CUDART__TYPES_1g0907ca7a1e7d0211b71ee49c5403072b) enums


In `cupy.cuda.Graph` class,
- `debug_dot_str(self, flags) -> str`: function to get debug DOT string

## Usage example

```python
import cupy
x = cupy.array([0, 1, 2, 3, 4])
y = cupy.array([5, 6, 7, 8, 9])
st = cupy.cuda.Stream()
with st:
    st.begin_capture()
    x = x + y
    g = st.end_capture()

print(g.debug_dot_str(cupy.cuda.runtime.cudaGraphDebugDotFlagsVerbose))
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8502/reactions","total_count":2,"+1":2,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8502/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/comments/2292850250","html_url":"https://github.com/cupy/cupy/pull/8502#issuecomment-2292850250","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8502","id":2292850250,"node_id":"IC_kwDOBFKgkM6Iqh5K","user":{"login":"so298","id":47357860,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ3MzU3ODYw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/47357860?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298","html_url":"https://github.com/so298","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-08-16T05:42:36Z","updated_at":"2024-08-16T05:42:36Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"@kmaehashi

Abstract

CUDA runtime provides a useful debug function [`cudaGraphDebugDotPrint()`](https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-runtime-api/group__CUDART__GRAPH.html#group__CUDART__GRAPH_1gbec177c250000405c570dc8c4bde20db) for CUDA graphs.
This function can generate [DOT format](https://graphviz.org/doc/info/lang.html) file from `cudaGraph_t`.

This PR contains CuPy's wrapper of this function with the following APIs:

In `cupy.cuda.runtime`, 
- `graphDebugDotPrint(graph, path, flags)` function
- [`cudaGraphDebugDotFlags`](https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-runtime-api/group__CUDART__TYPES.html#group__CUDART__TYPES_1g0907ca7a1e7d0211b71ee49c5403072b) enums


In `cupy.cuda.Graph` class,
- `debug_dot_str(self, flags) -> str`: function to get debug DOT string

## Usage example

```python
import cupy
x = cupy.array([0, 1, 2, 3, 4])
y = cupy.array([5, 6, 7, 8, 9])
st = cupy.cuda.Stream()
with st:
    st.begin_capture()
    x = x + y
    g = st.end_capture()

print(g.debug_dot_str(cupy.cuda.runtime.cudaGraphDebugDotFlagsVerbose))
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8502/reactions","total_count":2,"+1":2,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8502/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/comments/2293049190","html_url":"https://github.com/cupy/cupy/pull/8502#issuecomment-2293049190","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8502","id":2293049190,"node_id":"IC_kwDOBFKgkM6IrSdm","user":{"login":"so298","id":47357860,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ3MzU3ODYw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/47357860?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298","html_url":"https://github.com/so298","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-08-16T08:16:17Z","updated_at":"2024-08-16T08:16:17Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"@jakirkham

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8575","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8575/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8575/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8575/events","html_url":"https://github.com/cupy/cupy/issues/8575","id":2493514184,"node_id":"I_kwDOBFKgkM6UoAHI","number":8575,"title":"[RFC] Adding CUDA Graphs conditional node support with user-friendly APIs","user":{"login":"so298","id":47357860,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ3MzU3ODYw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/47357860?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298","html_url":"https://github.com/so298","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/so298/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-08-29T05:54:06Z","updated_at":"2024-08-29T05:54:06Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I’m working on CuPy’s CUDA Graph conditional node support and considering adding a more user-friendly graph constructing API to CuPy.  
I have two possible plans to achieve this goal:

- (A) “with” API
- (B) Functional API

## (A) “with” API

### pseudo code

```python
import cupy
from contextlib import contextmanager

# Add conditional node to capturing graph and
# switch to different stream to capture body graph
@contextmanager
def while_loop():
    ...
@contextmanager
def if_else():
    ...

a: cupy.ndarray = ...
b: cupy.ndarray = ...

with capture_context() as ctx:
    ...
    with ctx.while_loop( # while loop
        cond_fn=lambda: cupy.all(a == b),
        cond_fn_args=() # Optional
    ):
        ... # while loop body

    with ctx.if_else(cond_fn=lambda: cupy.all(a != b)) as cond:
        with cond.if_():
            ...
        with cond.else_():
            # Should we consider `else` body support?
            # Currently conditional node does not support `else`
            # but seems to add support in the near future.
            ...

graph = ctx.get_graph() # get captured graph
graph.launch()
```

### Pros

- Considering the construction of CUDA graphs through stream capture, this approach provides a natural API.
- Allows for more concise writing of nested conditional nodes.

### Cons

- Defining a loop using the “with” syntax in Python may not be intuitive.
- Adding a support for an "else" body can result in an awkward or unnatural syntax.

## (B) Functional API

Is this the same approach with [torch.\_higher_order_ops.while](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/blob/128544399441fa0ab32f2a95237212c911da5078/torch/_higher_order_ops/while_loop.py#L61-L114)?

### pseudo code

```python
# Prepare GraphConverter class to hold graph capturing state
class GraphConverter:
    def __init__(self):
        self.state = ... # need to have a global state

    # Function to define while loop
    def while_loop(self, cond_fn):
        def inner_func(body_fn, body_args=None):
            ... # operation to construct graph
        return inner_func

    def condition(self, cond_fn):
        def inner_func(true_fn, false_fn=None):
            ...
        return inner_func

    def convert(self, func):
        ...
        return func

gc = GraphConverter()
@gc.convert # A decorator to convert function to CUDA graph
def target(a: cupy.ndarray, b: cupy.ndarray):
    # While
    def while_fn(args):
        ...
    gc.while_loop(cond_fn=lambda: cupy.all(a == b))(while_fn, body_args)

    # You can also write as follows
    @gc.while_loop(cond_fn=lambda: cupy.all(a == b))
    def while_fn2():
        nonlocal a, b
        ...

    # if-then-else
    def true_fn():
        ...
    def false_fn():
        ...
    gc.condition(cond_fn)(
        true_fn=true_fn,
        false_fn=false_fn # easier to add `else` support
    )
    # or
    @gc.condition(cond_fn)
    def true_fn2():
        ...

target(a, b) # construct and execute graph at the same time
```

### Pros

- Simplifies the addition of "else" support when the conditional node supports an "else" body.
- Prevents issues related to unintentional variable overwriting and improper resource management, as functions naturally encapsulate variable scope.

### Cons

- Writing deeply nested programs can become complex and cumbersome, although using decorators can help alleviate this issue to some extent.
- Requires the use of the "nonlocal" declaration or passing variables as arguments to access outer-scope variables.

## Current status

I began implementing the API using the functional API approach (Plan B). However, switching to Plan A shouldn't be too time-consuming since the core implementation can be shared between both approaches.

I would like to ask your thoughts on these two ideas. Please let us know your preferences, potential advantages, pitfalls, and any other insights you might have.
